# Implementation Plan: Scoring HUD

**Branch**: `002-scoring-hud` | **Date**: 2026-05-16 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/002-scoring-hud/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit-plan`. Companion artifacts: [research.md](./research.md), [data-model.md](./data-model.md), [contracts/module-contracts.md](./contracts/module-contracts.md), [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md).

## Summary

Add a two-element DOM HUD over the WebGL canvas: a live score readout that ticks up by one point every 100 ms of running time, and an elapsed-run timer in `M:SS` / `MM:SS` format. Both indicators reset to zero on a new run, freeze when the run pauses (tab blur or visibility change), and resume on the player's un-pause input. Built as a thin slice over the runner-engine that already shipped in 001-lane-runner.

**Approach**: No new runtime state. Both readouts are *pure derived views* of `WorldState.tickMs` (already maintained by the existing `runner-engine` and already correctly paused on blur). A new pure-logic module `src/score/` exposes two formatter functions - one returns the integer score, one returns the formatted timer string - and is unit-tested in isolation. The game-loop reads them every frame and writes to two DOM elements (`#score`, `#timer`) inside a new `#hud` container in `index.html`. The HUD is styled in the same `<style>` block as the existing start screen and pause overlay so it inherits the project's typography.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x (`strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true`) - inherited from 001 unchanged.

**Primary Dependencies**: No new dependencies. Existing: `three`, `vite`, `typescript`, `vitest`, `eslint`, `typescript-eslint`, `@types/three`, `@types/node`, `jsdom`.

**Storage**: None for this slice (no persistence per spec).

**Testing**: Vitest in `node` environment for the new pure-logic tests (`src/score/score.test.ts`). No DOM/canvas needed - the score module returns plain values.

**Target Platform**: Same as 001 - modern evergreen browsers; mobile-first; 320 px minimum viewport for HUD legibility.

**Project Type**: Single project - static web app.

**Performance Goals**: Same 60 FPS desktop / >= 30 FPS mobile budget as 001. HUD update is two `Element.textContent` writes per frame; negligible overhead.

**Constraints**:
- Critical JS budget: <= 500 KB gzipped (current 133.90 KB; the new module adds maybe 1 KB).
- HUD legible on 320 px wide viewports; font size >= 16 CSS px at that width.
- HUD MUST NOT consume pointer events (so taps on the HUD area still drive the runner).
- Boundary: `src/score/` is a pure-logic module; it MUST NOT import `three` or any DOM types. The game-loop reads from the score module and writes to DOM elements; the score module never touches the DOM.

**Scale/Scope**: ~80 LOC of new logic + ~20 lines of test + ~30 lines of HTML/CSS + a few lines of wiring in `game-loop.ts`. Smallest slice we have planned.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

Evaluated against `.specify/memory/constitution.md` v1.0.0.

### Principle I - Simplicity & YAGNI

- [x] **No new dependencies.** The slice ships a pure-logic module and small HUD markup. Everything is already in the dep tree.
- [x] **No new state.** Score and timer are derived from `WorldState.tickMs`, not stored. This eliminates an entire class of "are these two values in sync?" bugs.
- [x] **No premature abstraction.** Two functions (`computeScore(tickMs)` and `formatTimer(tickMs)`) live in `src/score/`. No `IScoreProvider` interface, no event bus, no observable wrapper.

### Principle II - Test-First Discipline

- [x] **Pure-logic functions get unit tests written first.** `src/score/score.test.ts` covers: `computeScore(0) === 0`, `computeScore(99) === 0`, `computeScore(100) === 1`, `computeScore(199) === 1`, `computeScore(10_000) === 100` (the SC-002 invariant), monotonicity over a sampled range; and `formatTimer(0) === '0:00'`, `formatTimer(59_999) === '0:59'`, `formatTimer(60_000) === '1:00'`, `formatTimer(599_999) === '9:59'`, `formatTimer(600_000) === '10:00'`, and `formatTimer(5_999_999) === '99:59'` (the M:SS -> MM:SS boundary).
- [x] **Integration test.** The existing `tests/integration/lane-switch-flow.test.ts` gets a new case asserting that the score derived from `WorldState.tickMs` does NOT advance during the paused interval - validating spec FR-005.
- [x] **HUD DOM markup is exempt from strict TDD** (visual / view-layer, like the renderer). Manual validation in US1/US2 acceptance scenarios covers the visible HUD elements.

### Principle III - Library-First / Modular Design

- [x] **New `src/score/` module follows the established pattern.** Single public entrypoint (`src/score/index.ts`), pure logic only, no `three` import, no DOM import.
- [x] **ESLint boundary rule covers the new module by default.** `score/` is not in the allowlist for `three` imports, so the rule blocks any accidental dependency on Three.js or the renderer.
- [x] **Game-loop is the only integration point.** `game-loop.ts` reads from `score/` and writes to the DOM elements. `score/` knows nothing about either side.

### Principle IV - Observability & Debuggability

- [x] **The existing debug overlay** (`?debug=1`) already shows `tickMs` and other state - the same source of truth the HUD reads from. A discrepancy between debug overlay and HUD becomes immediately visible.
- [x] **No new console events needed.** State transitions (start, pause, resume) are already emitted by `runner-engine`. The score derives from those; adding parallel events would be noise.
- [x] **Errors include context** - the new functions are total and don't throw; nothing to log.

### Additional Constraints (Platform / Performance / Accessibility)

- [x] Static web app, no backend.
- [x] HUD CSS uses `pointer-events: none` so it doesn't capture taps - matches the existing start/pause overlay pattern.
- [x] HUD typography matches the existing overlays (system-ui font family inherited from `<body>`).
- [x] FR-008 ensures 320 px legibility via `font-size: clamp(...)`.

**Result**: ✅ All gates pass on initial check. No Complexity Tracking entries required.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/002-scoring-hud/
├── plan.md                 # This file
├── spec.md                 # Feature spec (already written)
├── research.md             # Phase 0 output - the few small decisions
├── data-model.md           # Phase 1 output - score + timer derivations
├── contracts/
│   └── module-contracts.md # Phase 1 output - the score/ module public API
├── quickstart.md           # Phase 1 output - delta over the 001 quickstart
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md     # Spec quality checklist (already passing)
└── tasks.md                # Phase 2 output (NOT created by /speckit-plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

This slice adds the `src/score/` module and the HUD DOM markup. Everything else in the tree from 001 is unchanged.

```text
geometry-dash/
├── index.html                       # adds <div id="hud">…</div> + CSS rules
├── src/
│   ├── score/                       # NEW pure-logic module
│   │   ├── index.ts                 # re-exports the two formatter functions
│   │   ├── score.ts                 # computeScore + formatTimer (+ small helpers)
│   │   └── score.test.ts            # unit tests, written first
│   ├── game/
│   │   └── game-loop.ts             # updated to grab #score and #timer elements,
│   │                                # call computeScore + formatTimer each frame,
│   │                                # and write textContent
│   └── main.ts                      # updated to also resolve #score and #timer
│                                    # by id and pass them to createGameLoop
└── tests/
    └── integration/
        └── lane-switch-flow.test.ts # one new case: score does not advance
                                     # while runState === 'paused'
```

No new module folders beyond `src/score/`. No deletions.

**Structure Decision**: Single-project static web app, unchanged from 001. The `src/score/` module sits alongside `src/lane-state/`, `src/runner-engine/`, `src/input-adapter/` - same shape, same boundary rules.

## Phase 0 - Outline & Research

See [research.md](./research.md). No new tech-stack decisions; this slice inherits everything from 001. Phase 0 records the small design choices specific to this slice:

- **Derive from `tickMs`** vs maintain a separate `ScoreState` (decision + rationale).
- **HUD position** (top corners vs top-centre) and the layout we picked.
- **Timer format** (M:SS / MM:SS, no hours, no decimals).
- **Why a pure module rather than an inline calculation in `game-loop.ts`** (testability boundary).

## Phase 1 - Design & Contracts

See [data-model.md](./data-model.md) and [contracts/module-contracts.md](./contracts/module-contracts.md).

- **Entities** are pure value types: a `ScoreReadout` (integer) and a `TimerReadout` (formatted string), both functions of `tickMs`.
- **Module contract** adds `src/score/` with two pure exports (`computeScore`, `formatTimer`) and a small `formatScore` helper. No new types beyond `number` and `string`.
- **Quickstart** updates the existing `quickstart.md` definition-of-done to include the new acceptance scenarios from `spec.md`.

## Post-Design Constitution Re-Check

*Re-evaluated after Phase 1.*

- [x] Module boundaries in `contracts/module-contracts.md` keep `src/score/` framework-free; `three` is still confined to `renderer/` + `game/`. **Principle III holds.**
- [x] Data model is two derivation functions; no new persistent state introduced. **Principle I holds.**
- [x] Test obligations declared upfront (boundary cases for `formatTimer`, `computeScore` arithmetic, monotonicity, paused-no-advance via integration). **Principle II holds.**
- [x] Observability inherits from the existing `runner-engine` events + debug overlay. **Principle IV holds.**

**Result**: ✅ Post-design re-check passes. Plan is ready to feed into `/speckit-tasks`.
